role_title: Team Analyst – Global Focused Growth Strategy

location.office_time_required: up to one day per week from home (implying ~4 days in office)

level_signals: Bachelor's degree and 4+ years of relevant buy-side/sell-side investment experience
Base salary range $180,000–$300,000 consistent with experienced analyst level
Apprenticeship framing suggests non-PM but senior enough for independent coverage

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree and 4+ years of relevant buy-side/sell-side investment experience.
Bachelor's degree and 4+ years of relevant buy-side/sell-side investment experience.
Advanced Excel and financial modeling capability, including three-statement builds and time-series analytics.
Strong academic foundation combining liberal-arts reasoning with quantitative training (e.g., calculus, statistics, econometrics; CFA coursework).
Working knowledge of equity markets and instruments.
Proven facility with AI tools and prompt engineering for research use cases.
Comfort operating in a high-intensity, performance-oriented team environment.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Self-directed, confident contributor who works independently and collaborates effectively.
Demonstrated passion for equity investing and market discovery.
Track record of converting deep research into clear, actionable insights.

explicitly_excluded: FINRA licenses are not required and will not be supported for this role.

scope.surface: Global Focused Growth Strategy — a complex, diversified global equity portfolio managed by Portfolio Manager Dave Eiswert
scope.business_model: active asset management, B2B and institutional
primary_metrics_inferred: Quality of investment recommendations / ex-ante vs. ex-post accuracy
Breadth and depth of research coverage
core_responsibilities: Adopt, execute, and continuously improve the Global Focused Growth investment framework (historical analysis, insight development, and forward modeling).
Lead scoped company/industry research projects and thematic workstreams aligned to portfolio needs.
Build and maintain three-statement and scenario models; synthesize market and alternative data into forecasts.
Partner with the Portfolio Manager and senior analysts to prepare briefs, update views, and translate research into portfolio actions.
Engage CEOs/CFOs and other external experts through meetings, calls, and road visits to test and refine theses.
Extremely detail oriented documentation, compliance discipline, and data hygiene; contribute to reusable templates and best practices.
Track ex-ante assumptions versus ex-post outcomes and participate in performance attribution reviews.
Conduct primary diligence (filings, transcripts, channel checks, site visits) to deepen company and industry understanding.
Develop investment recommendations over time and accept outcome accountability as responsibilities expand if appropriate.
Design and apply AI-driven workflows (prompt libraries, retrieval, automation) to improve research speed and quality.

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($180K–$300K base)
Named Portfolio Manager (Dave Eiswert) signals a specific, well-defined seat rather than a generic posting
Clear apprenticeship framing with long-term advancement path articulated
AI tooling explicitly embedded in role responsibilities — forward-looking
Broad benefits disclosed including hybrid work and annual bonus eligibility
Role covers truly global mandate, providing broad learning surface
red_flags: Hybrid policy is only one day per week from home — effectively near-full in-office commitment required
Very wide comp band ($180K–$300K) makes actual placement difficult to assess without knowing seniority ceiling
No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international candidates
FINRA licenses explicitly not supported, which may signal limited career mobility within financial services firms that require them

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (URL contains Baltimore-MD; body references Maryland as a comp jurisdiction)
ambiguities: The JD states the role 'is eligible for hybrid work, with up to one day per week from home' — this is a very limited hybrid arrangement closer to onsite. Classified as hybrid per stated policy but candidates should note ~4 days/week in office expectation.

Compensation note says 'Please review the job posting for the location of this specific opportunity' but then lists the same $180K–$300K range for all listed geographies (Maryland/Colorado/Washington/remote, DC, New York/California). It is unclear whether ranges actually differ by location or are uniform.

Remote workers are listed as eligible for the $180K–$300K range, but the hybrid policy elsewhere says up to one day from home — it is ambiguous whether fully remote is truly an option or only referenced for comp disclosure purposes.

CFA coursework appears in Required qualifications section under academic foundation, while 'CFA preferred' appears separately under Preferred — severity assigned as soft for standalone CFA designation, hard component inferred only for coursework exposure.
